Message.TypeName: Do not dispose objects multiple times
Message.Issue: Object 'fs' can be disposed more than once in method 'Attachments.UploadFile(Byte(), String, String)'. To avoid generating a System.ObjectDisposedException you should not call Dispose more than one time on an object.: Lines: 153

Message.TypeName: Review SQL queries for security vulnerabilities
Message.Issue: The query string passed to 'SqlDataAdapter.New(String, SqlConnection)' in 'ConnectOneWS.CheckAuthorization(ConnectOneWS.Permissions, String, String, String, ConnectOneWS.Tables, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param, Date)' could contain the following variables 'MyBase.ToString()'. If any of these variables could come from user input, consider using a stored procedure or a parameterized SQL query instead of building the query with string concatenations.

The query string passed to 'SqlDataAdapter.New(String, SqlConnection)' in 'ConnectOneWS.CheckAuthorization(ConnectOneWS.Permissions, String, String, String, ConnectOneWS.Tables, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param, Date)' could contain the following variables 'MyBase.ToString()', 'inBasicParam.screen.ToString()'. If any of these variables could come from user input, consider using a stored procedure or a parameterized SQL query instead of building the query with string concatenations.

'LandAndBuilding.Update_Insurance_Register(LandAndBuilding.Parameter_Update_Insurance_Register, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Delete_Magazine(String, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Delete_Magazine_Dispatch_Type(String, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Delete_Magazine_Dispatch_Type_Charges(String, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Delete_Magazine_Membership_Profile(String, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Delete_Magazine_Subscription_Type_Fee(String, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Update_Magazine_SubCity(Magazine.Parameter_Update_Magazine_Subcity, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'Magazine.Update_Magazine_Subs_Type_Fee(Magazine.Parameter_Update_Magazine_Subs_Type_Fee, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

'StockProfile.DeleteStockProfile(Integer, ConnectOneWS.Basic_Param)' rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

Implement IDisposable on 'Wrap_ExecuteFunctionDB' because it creates members of the following IDisposable types: 'SqlConnection'. If 'Wrap_ExecuteFunctionDB' has previously shipped, adding new members that implement IDisposable to this type is considered a breaking change to existing consumers.

Rule.TypeName: Do not dispose objects multiple times
Rule.Name: Do not dispose objects multiple times
Rule.Description: A correctly implemented Dispose method can be called multiple times without throwing an exception. However, this is not guaranteed and to avoid generating a System.ObjectDisposedException you should not call Dispose more than one time on an object.
Rule.Resolution: Object {0} can be disposed more than once in method {1}. To avoid generating a System.ObjectDisposedException you should not call Dispose more than one time on an object.: Lines: 5905

Rule.Name: Rethrow to preserve stack details
Rule.Description: Use the IL rethrow instruction to preserve original stack details when re-raising a caught exception.
Rule.Resolution: {0} rethrows a caught exception and specifies it explicitly as an argument. Use 'throw' without an argument instead, in order to preserve the stack location where the exception was initially raised.

Rule.TypeName: Review SQL queries for security vulnerabilities
Rule.Name: Review SQL queries for security vulnerabilities
Rule.Description: A SQL command string built from user input is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks. Microsoft SQL Server and other database servers support stored procedures and parameterized SQL queries, which reduce the risk of injection attacks.
Rule.Resolution: The query string passed to {0} in {1} could contain the following variables {2}. If any of these variables could come from user input, consider using a stored procedure or a parameterized SQL query instead of building the query with string concatenations.

Rule.Name: Types that own disposable fields should be disposable
Rule.Description: Types that declare disposable members should also implement IDisposable. If the type does not own any unmanaged resources, do not implement a finalizer on it.
Rule.Resolution: Implement IDisposable on {0} because it creates members of the following IDisposable types: {1}. If {0} has previously shipped, adding new members that implement IDisposable to this type is considered a breaking change to existing consumers.
